stemHtml: Prior to commencing the approach, the pilot of an aircraft says to the Air Traffic Services Unit (ATSU) at the planned destination aerodrome: "Please, call a taxi for us. We will arrive on schedule at 10:45." Can this be considered a Flight Regularity Message?

options.a: No, because individual requirements of passengers or crew shall NOT be admissible in this type of message.
options.b: Yes, because this is information for a representative of the aircraft`s operating agency and may be relayed by the ATSU.
options.c: No, because this type of message can only be passed once the aircraft is on the ground and passed onto the DELIVERY controller.
options.d: Yes, provided the ATSU has responsibility for ground handling services at the planned destination aerodrome.
